description_long: This object is a heavy, turret-mounted rotary cannon with a robust, industrial design, likely intended for automated or stationary defense in urban environments. The main body is a rectangular, armored housing with visible bolts and reinforced plating, supporting a multi-barreled rotary gun extending horizontally. The base is a wide, octagonal platform with four angular, flared stabilizing legs, suggesting it is designed to be fixed in place and withstand significant recoil. The barrels are arranged in a Gatling-style cluster, each with visible segmentations and a reinforced muzzle. The rear of the housing features mechanical details, including mounting brackets and possible access panels. The entire object is finished in a weathered, metallic gray, indicating heavy use and exposure to the elements.
description: A heavy, turret-mounted rotary cannon with a multi-barreled design and a reinforced, angular base, finished in weathered metallic gray.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rotary barrels and the rectangular armored housing atop the wide, angular base.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular arrangement of the barrels and the symmetrical, flared stabilizing legs of the base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the rotary barrels and the robust mounting structure.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the armored housing and the mechanical mounting, with the barrels receding into the distance.
